summary: The PoC demonstrates that the MCP client executes arbitrary commands without validation by spawning a subprocess that creates a proof-of-concept marker file, confirming complete command execution capabilities.
expected_security_impact: The PoC should create a marker file at /tmp/mcp_pwned via arbitrary command execution through the MCP client, demonstrating that attackers can execute any command with the privileges of the agent process.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh successfully spawned a subprocess via MCP() that created /tmp/mcp_pwned, confirming arbitrary command execution. The marker file was created with the agent's privileges, demonstrating full control over command execution.
verification_method: The run script checks for the existence of /tmp/mcp_pwned after execution. File existence confirms the MCP client spawned a subprocess that executed attacker-controlled commands, distinguishing real command execution from configuration errors or startup failures.
evidence_produced_by_run: Marker file /tmp/mcp_pwned created successfully via MCP subprocess spawn
File created with agent process privileges
No command validation or filtering blocked the malicious command
Environment variables passed through without filtering dangerous keys

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
